items.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
items.content: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

items.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says
items.content: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test

items.title: Apple moves to take its App Store fight back to the Supreme Court
items.content: Apple plans to ask the Supreme Court to review its App Store fight with Epic Games, as it challenges a ruling limiting its ability to charge fees on external payments.

items.title: Apple releases security fix for older iPhones and iPads to protect against DarkSword attacks
items.content: The security update protects a raft of older iPhones and iPads from attacks linked to leaked hacking tools called DarkSword.

items.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
items.content: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software

items.title: Developer of VeraCrypt encryption software says Windows users may face boot-up issues after Microsoft locked his account
items.content: The maker of the popular open source file encryption software VeraCrypt said Microsoft locked his online account, which may prevent device owners from booting up their compute

items.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
items.content: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

items.title: Russian government hackers broke into thousands of home routers to steal passwords
items.content: Fancy Bear, also known as APT28, has taken over thousands of residential home routers to steal passwords and authentication tokens in a wide-ranging espionage operation.

items.title: Judge Dismisses Trump’s Suit Against The Wall Street Journal
items.content: The judge said President Trump had not “plausibly alleged” that The Journal published the article with actual malice.

items.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
items.content: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is li

items.title: Hollywood Heavyweights Sign Letter Opposing Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros.
items.content: The letter warns that the deal will result in fewer jobs for creatives, along with higher costs and less choice for audiences.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Earnings Show Effects of Iran War
items.content: It will be a tricky earnings season, as the investment banking giant became the first to show on Monday.
